c.d: The maximum size of a single file that can be transferred using the app.
The average speed at which files are transferred between devices using the app.
The operating systems and devices supported by the app (e.g., Windows, macOS, Android, iOS).
c.s: Official app websites, app store listings

c.d: The security measures implemented by the app, such as end-to-end encryption, password protection, and compliance with standards like GDPR.

c.n: User Interface and Ease of Use
c.d: The intuitiveness and user-friendliness of the app's interface, affecting the ease with which users can send files.
The pricing structure of the app, including free tiers, subscription plans, and any associated costs.
c.s: Official app websites, app store listings

c.d: The app's ability to integrate with other platforms and services, such as email clients, cloud storage providers, and productivity tools.
The app's ability to transfer files between devices without requiring an internet connection.
The range of file types that can be transferred using the app, including documents, images, videos, and executables.
Any extra functionalities offered by the app, such as file synchronization, cloud backup, or media playback.
